hero_sub: AI-powered personal color analysis in seconds — find the palette that makes you glow.
home_feat1_desc: Upload your photo, get instant results
section_what_body1: Personal color analysis matches the colors of your clothing, makeup, and accessories to your natural skin tone, hair, and eye color. Based on seasonal color theory, everyone belongs to one of four seasons: Spring, Summer, Autumn, or Winter.
section_what_body2: Wearing your season's palette makes your complexion look vibrant and healthy — while the wrong colors can wash you out or make you appear tired.
banner_title: Ready to find your color season?
banner_sub: Upload a photo and get your personal color analysis in seconds.
analyzer_sub: Upload a clear, well-lit selfie to discover your personal color season and get tailored recommendations.
analyzer_browse: or click to browse · JPG, PNG, WEBP
analyzer_results_empty_sub: Upload a photo and click Analyze to get started
band_perfect_desc: {pct}% Excellent — these colors are an outstanding match for your skin tone.
band_strong_desc: {pct}% Excellent — these colors complement your complexion beautifully.
band_goodfit_desc: {pct}% Excellent — these colors work reasonably well with your skin tone.
band_decent_desc: {pct}% Good — moderate compatibility. Some adjustments could improve the match.
band_clash_desc: {pct}% Fair — significant color clash detected with your skin tone.
band_fair_desc: {pct}% Fair — slight clashing detected. Season-matched colors are recommended.
reco_perfect_intro: A {pct}% Excellent score means these colors are exceptionally well-suited to your {season} complexion — your choices are outstanding.
reco_strong_intro: {pct}% Excellent — these colors work beautifully for your {season} complexion. Your choices are well-suited to your undertone.
reco_decent_intro: Your colors show {pct}% Excellent compatibility with your {season} complexion. A few targeted adjustments could bring this significantly higher.
reco_good_intro: {pct}% Good compatibility detected for your {season} skin tone. Small palette adjustments could push this into the Excellent range.
reco_clash_intro: A {pct}% Fair score indicates these colors clash noticeably with your {season} skin tone. Switching to season-matched shades could visibly transform your look.
reco_fair_intro: {pct}% Fair — these colors have some clashing with your {season} undertone. Switching to better-matched shades would noticeably enhance your complexion.
reco_tip_perfect1: Your {season} undertone absolutely loves these colors — keep wearing this palette
reco_tip_perfect2: Add accessories in similar tones to extend the harmonious effect
reco_tip_perfect3: Try bolder or more saturated versions of these shades for special occasions
reco_tip_strong1: These shades align well with your {season} undertone — wear them confidently
reco_tip_strong2: Combine the best-performing tones together for maximum polish
reco_tip_strong3: Explore deeper or lighter variations to add dimension without losing harmony
reco_tip_decent1: Compare your current shades with the recommended {season} palette
reco_tip_decent2: Focus on tones that score highest in Excellent for future outfit choices
reco_tip_decent3: Avoid shades that skew too far from your undertone
reco_tip_good1: Shift gradually toward your core {season} palette for more vibrant results
reco_tip_good2: Pair the best-matching shades together to maximize visual impact
reco_tip_good3: Use the recommended color swatches below as a starting reference
reco_tip_clash1: Swap these colors for shades from your {season} palette
reco_tip_clash2: Avoid colors that compete directly with your undertone, especially near your face
reco_tip_clash3: Take the Color Quiz for a complete personal color season guide
reco_tip_fair2: Try softer or warmer/cooler variations of your current shades first
reco_tip_fair3: Take the Color Quiz for deeper guidance on your personal color season
do4: No heavy filters or color corrections
q1_text: Look at the veins on both wrists. What color do they appear most clearly?
q2_text: Which direction does your skin tone lean?
q3_text: After spending time in the sun, what color does your skin turn?
q4_text: Is your skin tone more dark or light?
q5_text: How dark is your natural hair color?
spring_desc: Spring types suit bright, vivid colors full of life — like nature in bloom. You give off an approachable, friendly, and energetic vibe.
summer_desc: Summer types suit soft, muted, romantic colors — like morning mist. You give off an elegant and refined impression.
autumn_desc: Autumn types suit warm, deep, rich earth tones — like falling leaves. You give off a grounded, trustworthy, and mature presence.
winter_desc: Winter types suit bold, sharp, high-contrast colors — like a cold winter day. You give off a modern, striking, and sophisticated vibe.
guide_subtitle: Build your perfect look, season by season.
guide_tip1_desc: Makeup looks best on well-hydrated skin
guide_tip2_desc: Build coverage gradually for a natural finish
guide_tip3_desc: No harsh lines — blend every product thoroughly
spring_step1_desc: Apply a lightweight hydrating primer. Spring types glow with dewy, fresh-looking skin — avoid matte finishes.
spring_step1_tip: Use a vitamin C serum underneath for extra radiance.
spring_step2_desc: Choose a warm-toned foundation (golden or peachy undertone). Shade: 1–2 levels lighter than your deepest skin tone.
spring_step2_tip: Blend with a damp beauty sponge for a natural finish.
spring_step3_desc: Best shades: coral, peach, warm pink. Apply to the apples of cheeks and blend upward toward temples.
spring_step3_tip: Spring types look best with a flushed, just-pinched look.
spring_step4_desc: Warm browns, terracotta, champagne gold, soft olive. Avoid cool greys or dark charcoal.
spring_step4_tip: Tight-line with brown eyeliner instead of black.
spring_step5_desc: Best colors: coral red, warm nude, peachy pink, tomato red. Avoid blue-based reds or cool mauves.
spring_step5_tip: Top with a clear gloss for that Spring freshness.
spring_step6_desc: Dewy setting spray only — never heavy powder. Spring types need to maintain luminosity.
summer_step1_desc: Lightweight moisturizer + blurring primer. Summer types suit a soft-focus, porcelain skin look.
summer_step1_tip: Apply a lavender color-correcting primer to neutralize any redness.
summer_step2_desc: Choose a cool or neutral-toned foundation with a pink or rosy undertone. Avoid orange or yellow-based formulas.
summer_step2_tip: Build coverage lightly — Summer types look best with skin showing through.
summer_step3_desc: Dusty rose, soft mauve, cool pink, lavender blush. Apply softly on cheekbones — Summer suits a delicate flush.
summer_step3_tip: Try a cream blush for a natural, watercolor effect.
summer_step4_desc: Soft taupe, lavender, dusty rose, slate blue, mauve. Avoid warm browns or gold — they look muddy on cool skin.
summer_step4_tip: Brown-black mascara instead of jet black looks softer and more natural.
summer_step5_desc: Best: dusty rose, raspberry, cool nude, berry, soft fuchsia. Avoid orange-based or warm nudes — they wash you out.
summer_step5_tip: A tinted lip balm in rose gives a perfectly effortless Summer look.
summer_step6_desc: Light pressed powder in a translucent pink-toned formula. Avoid yellow-based powders.
autumn_step1_desc: Rich moisturizer + satin-finish primer. Autumn types suit a warm, healthy-looking skin texture.
autumn_step1_tip: Add a drop of facial oil to foundation for depth and warmth.
autumn_step2_desc: Deep golden, warm beige, or amber-toned foundation. Autumn types suit the richest warm shades beautifully.
autumn_step2_tip: Use a flat brush to pack coverage — Autumn suits a more polished finish.
autumn_step3_desc: Terracotta, warm brick, deep peach, earthy coral. Apply more heavily than Spring — Autumn suits a sculpted flush.
autumn_step3_tip: Dust bronzer first, then blush on top for dimension.
autumn_step4_desc: Burnt sienna, chocolate brown, olive green, deep gold, rust. Autumn eyes are meant to be smoky and rich.
autumn_step4_tip: Smudge a deep brown along the lower lash line for a sultry look.
autumn_step5_desc: Best: burnt orange, deep terracotta, warm brown, brick red, caramel. Avoid cool pinks or blue-based purples.
autumn_step5_tip: Line lips slightly outside the natural line for fullness.
autumn_step6_desc: Warm-toned setting powder with a satin finish. A light dusting of bronzer to set = perfect Autumn glow.
winter_step1_desc: Hydrating primer with a luminous finish. Winter types suit porcelain, glass-like skin.
winter_step1_tip: Apply a blue or purple color corrector under eyes for brightness.
winter_step2_desc: Cool-toned, high-coverage foundation — pink, neutral, or cool beige. Winter skin can handle full coverage beautifully.
winter_step2_tip: Contour with a cool grey-brown, never an orange-toned bronzer.
winter_step3_desc: Deep rose, cool fuchsia, berry, icy pink. Apply on the cheekbones only — sharp and defined, not diffused.
winter_step3_tip: Winter types can wear bold blush — don't be afraid of colour.
winter_step4_desc: Navy, charcoal, cool grey, deep plum, icy silver, black. Winter eyes are dramatic and high-contrast.
winter_step4_tip: Tight-line with jet black for a striking, defined look.
winter_step5_desc: Best: classic red, deep berry, cool fuchsia, dark plum, blue-red. Winter types can wear the boldest lip colors of all seasons.
winter_step5_tip: A true blue-red lipstick is the signature Winter look.
winter_step6_desc: Translucent or cool-toned setting powder. Finish with a light illuminating mist for glass skin effect.
